definitions.definition: Raw data collected by the investigator himself through a survey or experiment. It is a first-hand information. It is original and primary, collected from primary sources or original sources.
Data collected by someone else rather than the investigator. It is a second-hand information. It is already collected, compiled and analyzed by someone else.
A collection of numerical facts, figures and dates that are related to the study of economics.
The process of presenting data in the form of a table.
A range of values between two numbers, used to group data into categories.
The lower and upper values of a class interval.
The value in the middle of a class interval.
The number of times a particular value occurs in a dataset.
A table that shows the number of times each value occurs in a dataset.
A graphical representation of a frequency distribution, showing the number of times each value occurs.
A graphical representation of data using bars, showing the relationship between different categories.
A circular chart that shows how different categories contribute to a whole.
A graphical representation of data using a line, showing the relationship between different categories over time.
A graphical representation of data showing the relationship between two variables.
The average value of a dataset, calculated by adding up all the values and dividing by the number of values.
The middle value of a dataset, used to represent the average value.
The most frequently occurring value in a dataset.
The difference between the highest and lowest values in a dataset.
Data that can be measured or counted, such as numbers or amounts.
Data that cannot be measured or counted, such as opinions or descriptions.
